fundamentals.name: Datadog, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Datadog, Inc. operates an observability and security platform for cloud applications in the United States and internationally. The company's products comprise infrastructure and application performance monitoring, log management, observability pipelines, synthetics, real user monitoring, product analytics, continuous profiler, database monitoring, data observability, LLM observability, error tracking, network monitoring, incident response, workflow automation and App builder, event management, bits AI SRE, cloud cost management, cloud security, code security, cloud SIEM, threat management, sensitive data scanner, and CI visibility. Datadog, Inc. was incorporated in 2010 and is headquartered in New York, New York.

judgment.summary: High-growth company with impressive revenue and earnings expansion, but trades at a premium valuation with thin operating margins.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates with EPS of $0.60 vs $0.51; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 32.2% YoY and quarterly earnings growth 113.4% indicate strong business momentum.
Forward P/E of 79.64 is lower than trailing P/E of 566.64, suggesting expected earnings growth.
Gross margin of 79.9% is very strong, indicating efficient core operations.
Free cash flow of $936.73M is substantial.
Analyst consensus is 'strong_buy' with a mean target of $223.93, suggesting upside potential.
negatives: Operating margin of 0.8% and profit margin of 3.7% are very thin, indicating high operating costs.
Return on equity of 3.9% and return on assets of -0.2% are weak.
Price/Sales (TTM) of 21.97 is high, suggesting a rich valuation.
Debt/Equity ratio of 32.22, while current and quick ratios are healthy, indicates significant leverage relative to equity.

news.paragraph: Datadog (DDOG) is seeing mixed analyst sentiment, with JPMorgan highlighting its AI-driven growth and a Yahoo Finance article suggesting it's a buy when AI goes into production, while Goldman Sachs maintains a sell rating despite raising its target. Recent reports indicate DDOG is outperforming Microsoft and Adobe in 2026, and it recently achieved its first $1 billion quarter with an improved 2026 outlook. The company's strong earnings growth and revenue expansion are notable, though operating and profit margins remain thin.

headlines.title: This AI Stock Is Crushing Microsoft And Adobe In 2026 — JPMorgan Still Sees 43% Upside
